name: Fedorov Avtomat
description: The Fedorov Avtomat Model 1916 is considered one of the world’s first weapons close in concept to modern assault rifles. It was developed by Russian designer Vladimir Grigoryevich Fedorov during World War I.

name: PPD-34
description: The PPD-34, Degtyaryov submachine gun, was an early Soviet submachine gun created by Vasily Alekseyevich Degtyaryov and adopted in 1934. It used the 7.62×25 mm TT cartridge and had a relatively high rate of fire. Compared with foreign counterparts of the time, the PPD-34 was distinguished by high reliability and a fairly simple design.

name: PPSh-41
description: The PPSh-41, Shpagin submachine gun, was a Soviet automatic weapon developed by Georgy Shpagin in 1941 as a cheaper and more technologically efficient replacement for the PPD-40. It used the 7.62×25 mm cartridge and operated from an open bolt. It was distinguished by a high rate of fire and reliability in combat conditions. Soviet soldiers respectfully called the PPSh-41 “Papasha”.

name: AK-47
description: The AK-47 was the first production Kalashnikov assault rifle, developed for the armed forces of the USSR. It is distinguished by high reliability, simplicity of design, and effectiveness in combat conditions.

name: AKM
description: The AKM is a modernized version of the AK-47 with a lighter design, improved ergonomics, and a muzzle compensator to reduce recoil. It became the main assault rifle of the Soviet Army for several decades.

name: AK-74
description: The AK-74 was the main assault rifle of the Soviet Army from the late 1970s. It has lower recoil and better accuracy compared with the AKM thanks to its reduced caliber and new design solutions.

name: AKS-74U
description: The AKS-74U is a compact version of the AK-74 assault rifle, created for combat vehicle crews, reconnaissance units, and special forces. Despite its reduced dimensions, it retains high reliability and is fairly effective at short ranges.

name: AK-74M
description: The AK-74M is a modernized version of the AK-74. Its main differences include a reinforced design, a side-folding impact-resistant plastic stock, standardized optics mounts, and improved ergonomics. Thanks to its high reliability, low recoil, and good accuracy, it became the standard weapon of the Russian army and special forces.

name: Mosin Rifle Model 1891
description: The Mosin Rifle Model 1891 was one of Russia’s most widely produced and famous rifles, adopted in 1891. It was used in the Russo-Japanese War, World War I, and the Russian Civil War. It was distinguished by reliability, good firing range, and high stopping power. It had a 5-round magazine.

name: Mosin Rifle Model 1891/30
description: The Mosin Rifle Model 1891/30 was a modernized version of the Mosin rifle with an improved sight, shortened barrel, and more convenient design. It was actively used in the Great Patriotic War, especially as a sniper weapon.

name: SVT-40
description: The SVT-40 was one of the first mass-produced semi-automatic rifles in the USSR. It was used during the Great Patriotic War, but because of production and maintenance complexity it was partially replaced by the Mosin rifle and PPSh-41. It was distinguished by a high rate of fire and good ballistics, but required careful handling.

name: SKS
description: The SKS was a compact, convenient, and reliable carbine intended to replace the Mosin rifle, but it was soon displaced by the AK-47. It was used in the 1950s and later remained in the armies of the socialist bloc as a ceremonial and training weapon.

name: SVD
description: The SVD was the world’s first mass-produced semi-automatic sniper rifle. It is reliable and convenient. It is still used by the Russian army today. It is distinguished by high accuracy, but is not intended for high-precision long-range shooting.

name: SV-98
description: The SV-98 is a modern high-precision sniper rifle intended for army and special forces units. It uses a bolt-action mechanism and 10-round magazines. It is distinguished by high accuracy and the ability to use various optical sights.

name: Maxim Machine Gun
description: The Maxim machine gun was one of the first fully automatic machine guns, developed by Hiram Maxim. It used recoil energy for reloading and had a water-cooled barrel. It was used in World War I, the Russian Civil War, and many other conflicts.

name: Maxim Machine Gun Model 1910
description: The Maxim Machine Gun Model 1910 was a Russian modernization of the Maxim machine gun, equipped with a water-cooling jacket and Sokolov wheeled mount. It was used in both world wars and in the Russian Civil War.

name: DP-27
description: The DP-27 was a Soviet light machine gun that used a 47-round pan magazine. It was distinguished by simple design and reliability. It was the main infantry machine gun of the Red Army during World War II.

name: SG-43
description: The SG-43 was a Soviet mounted machine gun that replaced the outdated Maxim Machine Gun Model 1910. It used belt feeding and air cooling and was distinguished by greater mobility.

name: RPD
description: The RPD was the first Soviet light machine gun chambered for the intermediate 7.62×39 mm cartridge. It used belt feeding, which provided greater firing autonomy. It served as the basis for later developments, including the RPK.

name: PK
description: The PK is a Soviet general-purpose machine gun developed by Mikhail Kalashnikov. It is distinguished by high reliability and is used on a mount and as armament for combat vehicles.

name: PKM
description: The PKM is an improved version of the PK with reduced weight and improved ergonomics. It is still the main general-purpose machine gun in the Russian army.

name: PKP Pecheneg
description: The PKP Pecheneg is a Russian modernized machine gun based on the PKM, with a fixed barrel and an improved cooling system that allows sustained fire without overheating. It is used by Russian special forces and the army.

name: MP18
description: The MP 18, Maschinenpistole 18, was a submachine gun intended to arm special assault detachments as well as police forces. It was patented in December 1917 by designer Hugo Schmeisser, whose development of the new submachine gun was financially supported by Theodor Bergmann.

name: MP28
description: The MP28 was a submachine gun that continued the MP18 concept, distinguished by an improved design and higher-capacity magazines. Although it did not become as widely famous as the later MP38/40, it was a notable example of a German submachine gun for its decade.

name: MP 38 / 40
description:
The MP 38/40, abbreviation of the German Maschinenpistole, was a submachine gun developed by Heinrich Vollmer. It served with the Wehrmacht during World War II.
The MP 40 was a modification of the MP 38 submachine gun. Like the MP 38, the MP 40 was intended primarily for tank crews, motorized infantry, paratroopers, and infantry squad commanders. Later, closer to the end of the war, it began to be used quite widely by German infantry, although it was never universally issued.

name: StG44
description:
The StG 44, German Sturmgewehr 44, was a German assault rifle developed during World War II. Among modern-type assault rifles, it became the first design to be mass-produced, with about 450 thousand units manufactured.
It differed from World War II submachine guns, such as the PPSh, MP 40, and others, by having a significantly greater aimed firing range, mainly thanks to the use of a so-called intermediate cartridge, which was more powerful and had better ballistics than the pistol cartridges used in submachine guns.

name: HK G3
description: The HK G3 is a rifle developed based on the Spanish CETME rifle, but with a number of improvements. It became the main rifle of the Bundeswehr for several decades and was exported to more than 60 countries.

name: HK MP5
description: The HK MP5 is one of the most famous submachine guns of the 20th century, becoming a symbol of police and special forces units around the world. The MP5 gained enormous popularity and was widely exported outside Germany.

name: HK G36
description:
The Heckler & Koch Gewehr 36, G36, is a family of small arms developed in the early 1990s by the German company Heckler & Koch under the internal designation HK 50 to replace the well-known HK G3 automatic rifle.
From a design standpoint, the weapon’s mechanism is a variant of the AR-18 rifle, developed in the early 1960s in the United States, but made with extensive use of modern materials, including high-strength polymers for the receiver.

name: Mauser 98
description: The Mauser Gewehr 98, Mauser 98, is a magazine-fed rifle Model 1898 developed by German designer Paul Mauser. Thanks to features such as high accuracy, powerful fire, and reliability, the rifle is still widely used as a hunting and sporting weapon, as well as a sniper rifle in local wars.

name: Karabiner 98b
description: The Karabiner 98b was a shortened version of the Gewehr 98 rifle, developed for the Reichswehr after World War I. Despite the name “carbine”, it was a long-barreled rifle, with a barrel length of 740 mm, because the Treaty of Versailles forbade Germany from developing and producing short carbines.

name: Mauser 98k
description: The Mauser 98k is a magazine-fed rifle, known in German sources as Karabiner 98k, Kar98k, or K98k, officially adopted in 1935. It was the main and most widely produced small arm of the Wehrmacht during World War II. Structurally, it is a shortened and slightly modified version of the Mauser 98 rifle.

name: Gewehr 43
description: The Gewehr 43 was a German 7.92 mm semi-automatic rifle of World War II chambered for the 7.92×57 mm cartridge, and was a modification of the earlier Gewehr 41 rifle with a modified gas system similar to that of the Soviet SVT-40 rifle.

name: HK G3
description: The HK G3 is a rifle developed based on the Spanish CETME rifle, but with a number of improvements. It became the main rifle of the Bundeswehr for several decades and was exported to more than 60 countries.

name: HK G36
description:
The Heckler & Koch Gewehr 36, G36, is a family of small arms developed in the early 1990s by the German company Heckler & Koch under the internal designation HK 50 to replace the well-known HK G3 automatic rifle.
From a design standpoint, the weapon’s mechanism is a variant of the AR-18 rifle, developed in the early 1960s in the United States, but made with extensive use of modern materials, including high-strength polymers for the receiver.

name: MG 08
description: The MG 08, German Maschinengewehr 08, was a 7.9 mm variant of the Maxim heavy machine gun, produced in Germany from 1908 and used in World War I. By the beginning of World War II, the MG 08 was already an obsolete weapon, and its use in the Wehrmacht was explained only by a shortage of more modern machine guns.

name: MG 13
description: The MG 13, German Maschinengewehr 08, was a German light machine gun developed on the basis of Louis Schmeisser’s MG 10 machine gun, German patent No. 192508 with priority from February 13, 1906, by a team of gunsmiths, technologists, and engineers from Rheinmetall’s machine-gun development department headed by Louis Stange.

name: MG 34
description: The MG 34, German Maschinengewehr 13, was a German general-purpose machine gun created before World War II.

name: MG 42
description: The MG 42, German Maschinengewehr 42, was a German general-purpose machine gun of World War II. It was developed by Metall-und Lackwarenfabrik Johannes Großfuß in 1942. Because of its high rate of fire, it received nicknames among Soviet front-line soldiers and the Allies such as “Bone Cutter”, “Hitler’s Buzzsaw”, “Emga”, and “Cross”.

name: MG3
description: The MG3 is a German general-purpose machine gun. It is an improved variant of the MG 42 machine gun.

name: Thompson M1921
description: The Thompson M1921 was one of the first submachine guns in history. It was used by police, gangsters, and the army. It was very reliable, but heavy.

name: M2 Carbine
description: The M2 Carbine was the automatic version of the M1 Carbine rifle. It was used as an auxiliary weapon for crews, officers, and weapon teams.

name: M14
description: The M14 was a universal weapon that replaced the Garand. Excessively strong recoil made it difficult to fire effectively in bursts.

name: M16
description: The M16 was the first rifle to make extensive use of plastic parts. It was lightweight, highly accurate, and used in Vietnam.

name: M16A1
description: The M16A1 was a version with improved reliability. Chrome-plated parts and a flash suppressor were added.

name: M16A2
description: The M16A2 featured a new barrel system, a heavier barrel, an adjustable sight, and a changed fire mode.

name: M4
description: The M4 is a shortened version of the M16A2, created for urban combat and confined spaces.

name: M1903 Springfield
description: The M1903 Springfield was the main U.S. rifle in World War I. It was reliable and accurate.

name: M1917 Enfield
description: The M1917 Enfield was the American version of the British P14. It was a cheaper and simpler alternative to the Springfield.

name: M1 Garand
description: The M1 Garand was the army’s first mass-produced semi-automatic rifle. It was used in World War II and Korea.

name: M14
description: The M14 was a universal weapon that replaced the Garand. Excessively strong recoil made it difficult to fire effectively in bursts.

name: M16
description: The M16 was the first rifle to make extensive use of plastic parts. It was lightweight, highly accurate, and used in Vietnam.

name: M16A1
description: The M16A1 was a version with improved reliability. Chrome-plated parts and a flash suppressor were added.

name: M16A2
description: The M16A2 featured a new barrel system, a heavier barrel, an adjustable sight, and a changed fire mode.

name: M4
description: The M4 is a shortened version of the M16A2, created for urban combat and confined spaces.

name: Colt–Browning M1895
description: The Colt–Browning M1895 was the first American machine gun and was used in the Spanish-American War and World War I.

name: Colt–Browning M1917
description: The Colt–Browning M1917 was a heavy water-cooled machine gun. It was used on all fronts of World War I and World War II.

name: Browning M2
description: The Browning M2 is a heavy machine gun still in use today. It is effective against light vehicles, aircraft, and fortifications.

name: M60
description: The M60 is a general-purpose infantry machine gun. It was actively used in Vietnam. Its nickname was “The Pig”.

name: M249
description: The M249 is a light machine gun for fire support within an infantry squad. It is fed from a belt or magazine.

name: Sten Gun
description: The Sten Gun was a simple and cheap submachine gun used by British troops during World War II.

name: L1A1
description: The L1A1 is the British version of the FN FAL, a semi-automatic rifle and the main weapon of the United Kingdom in the second half of the 20th century.

name: L85A1
description: The L85A1 has been the main assault rifle of the British Army since 1985 and is part of the SA80 family.

name: Lee-Enfield Mk I
description: The Lee-Enfield Mk I was the first version of the Lee-Enfield rifle and the standard British rifle until 1907.

name: SMLE Mk III
description: The SMLE Mk III was the main rifle of the British Army in World War I.

name: SMLE Mk III
description: The SMLE Mk III was the main rifle of the British Army in World War I.

name: Lee-Enfield No.4 Mk I
description: The Lee-Enfield No.4 Mk I was a modernized SMLE Mk III rifle and was widely used in World War II.

name: L1A1 SLR
description: The L1A1 SLR is the British version of the FN FAL, a semi-automatic rifle and the main weapon of the United Kingdom in the second half of the 20th century.

name: L85A1
description: The L85A1 has been the main assault rifle of the British Army since 1985 and is part of the SA80 family.

name: Maxim Gun
description: The Maxim Gun was the world’s first fully automatic machine gun and was used by the British Army from the late 19th century.

name: Vickers Machine Gun
description: The Vickers Machine Gun was a heavy mounted machine gun widely used in both world wars.

name: Lewis Gun
description: The Lewis Gun was the first light machine gun of the British Army and was used in World War I and World War II.

name: Bren Gun
description: The Bren Gun was the main light machine gun of British infantry in World War II.

name: Bren Mk II
description: The Bren Mk II was a simplified version of the Bren Mk I for faster production.

name: Bren L4
description: The Bren L4 was a modification of the Bren machine gun chambered for the NATO cartridge.

name: GPMG (L7)
description: The GPMG (L7) is the British version of the FN MAG machine gun, a general-purpose machine gun.
